events.note: ComfyUI v0.27->v0.28 wave COMPLETE + user-approved for done. All 5 phases verified: pins (4 sites), dev image build both legs green, MPI-341's 4 checks, MPI-340 build-gated dev-tag leg (both legs pulled live), workflow sweep on 0.28. Stable pins untouched; released users unaffected.

Pod build hardening COMPLETE + user-approved for done. All 4 checks: smoke layer green on clean dev build, unpin-kornia proof RED at smoke layer (LTXVideo IMPORT FAILED, run 30028617382), +cu130 trio + constraints.txt in final image, one LTX gen on dev Pod. The load-bearing grep gate is proven to bite.

Scope extended (user): the dev/stable split must land in docs, rules and skills, not just code. Grep-verified blast radius added to brief: CLAUDE.md:74 (Context Router row - highest-traffic instruction, currently teaches 'publish-runtime.sh stable' as THE way to ship a runtime edit), docs/runpod-remote-engine.md s5+s6, docs/builder/02-image-and-rebuild.md:100, .claude/commands/build-pod-image.md:123+94, .claude/rules/comfy_engine.md:291, docs/runpod-troubleshooting.md (4 mentions), mpi-ci/cubric-vision-pod/README.md 'Runtime externalize', plus a dos_and_donts.md one-liner. KEY FINDING: mpi-release/SKILL.md and mpi-version-bump/SKILL.md have ZERO Pod awareness today (grep-verified) - harmless with one channel, but this card creates the hazard, so mpi-release gains a Preconditions drift check (curl dev vs stable manifest.json, compare the sha256 fields they already carry) that STOPS and asks the user to promote or confirm deliberate non-shipping. Never auto-promote in the release flow - live op affecting released users, same class as git push. mpi-version-bump deliberately unchanged: app version and Pod runtime are separate artifacts on separate cadences.

Moved to TOP of todo (user) - MPI-340 unblocks MPI-333/MPI-329 (both likely land fixes in wrapper.py, which currently publishes straight to the 'stable' channel released users boot). Brief extended: the dev/user split for wrapper.py + start.sh is the CHANNEL, not duplicate wrapper.dev.py files (git versions the file, the channel versions the deploy). Added a 'publish-runtime.sh promote' verb that rclone-copies vision/dev/* -> vision/stable/* server-side so promotion ships the EXACT bytes tested on dev, refusing on a working-tree/dev sha mismatch (the script already emits per-file sha256 in manifest.json). Runtime promote and image bump are independent paths.

Dev Pod images (GPU + CPU) + dev R2 runtime channel, both gated on the EXISTING server-side _devMode (remotePodLifecycle.js:37). 1.1.0 is released and every released app pins the same image tag AND the same 'stable' runtime channel, so publish-runtime.sh stable reaches real users on their next Pod boot. No new Dockerfile, no CI change (manifest_version=0.17.0-dev already yields v0.17.0-dev-cu130). Safety substrate for MPI-333 / MPI-329 / MPI-341.

Adopt two build practices from YanWenKun/ComfyUI-Docker: (1) RUN --quick-test-for-ci --cpu after the node bake so an import failure fails the BUILD (the MPI-149 kornia/LTXVideo class that shipped and died live as Node Stage1_Bypass not found; also automates the hand-run '8 nodes register' gate from MPI-139); (2) pip constraints file pinning the cu130 trio so torch drift is unresolvable, with the MPI-244 assert kept as the belt. Blocked on MPI-340 - build to the dev tag, never rebuild released v0.16.0.

Publish the 1.1.0 GitHub release - prep DONE + delta update proven live; only tag-move + Gate 2 + publish remain. Deferred to a later session (user). Run mpi-release.

RunPod validation sweep COMPLETE (1.1.0 release gate). MPI-282/300/310 remote-proven, MPI-326 fix verified; Head Swap residual tracked on MPI-299/306/325. Closed at 1.1.0 release close-out.
Public 1.1.0 changelog aggregation shipped; version-rename landed on master; drag-drop-import wording corrected to LoRA/upscale only. release:check green.

Removing a non-last image chip may strand the required Input_Image -> Qwen-Edit silently returns no output. Local engine healthy, NOT the MPI-326 fix. Primary hypothesis: chip-slot repack (project_media_slots_addressable_never_packed); secondary: stale Pod path. Deterministic repro + code entry points in card. Non-blocking for 1.1.

Remote PASS (RTX PRO 4000, MPI-324 sweep). Full shared-dep guard lifecycle: inverse-GC kept encoder on Krea uninstall (2->1 holder), describe from gallery+history landed caption, negative-flip correct, uninstall freed 5.24GB (104.2->99GB) + row flipped to Install after refresh. Both engine twins proven. Core deliverable + plugin entity + GC guard complete; stale destructive-bug attention cleared. Deferred step5 video-describe=MPI-287, plugin-row download-mode race=MPI-320.

Remote PASS (RTX PRO 4000 Pod, MPI-324 sweep). Tier radio (all 3), 2-image combine (Head Swap), 3rd chip Input_Image_3 (injects local + remote - chip-3-unique blue-hair girl landed in qwenEdit_005 remote AND qwenEdit_003 local; graph wiring traced correct not crossed), style rack (3 styles remote). Caveat: scene + 2 distinct subjects in one pass is model-unstable (keeps one, morphs other) - same family as Krea2 2-subject limit, mitigation = chain per MPI-313. Injection correct; instability is the transformer.

Perf: remote cold model-load ~2min per swap vs ~14s warm (RTX PRO 4000, MPI-324 sweep). Per-model cold cost, consistent Krea2/Chroma. User caveat: may just be a weak inference card - retest on mainstream GPU. Not a 1.1 blocker.

Observed 1.1.1 CPU download Pod (booting wrapper -> 5 models falsely installed, no download). Lines present in 1.2.0. Adjacent to MPI-326. Fix: default false + guard every.

RunPod remote pass (MPI-324 sweep, RTX 2000). Krea2 t2i/i2i/depth/upscale/masked edit + 2-character edit all clean; accel LoRA resolved (no silent degrade); backslash LoRA path worked on Linux. Step count result-verified not console-counted.

Remote op-dropdown flashes + auto-closes on a ~1s models:checked rebuild storm. Defect B confirmed (PromptBox _refreshOpDropdown rebuilds unconditionally; Model Library already guards the same). Defect A hypothesis (remote:connection connected-edge flapping during Pod init re-fires syncModelInstalled). Surfaced in MPI-324 sweep. Scope 1.2-blocker vs 1.3 pending user.

Remote-proven on RTX 2000 Pod (MPI-324 sweep). Wrapper reinstalled MpiNodes aaa1d2d on connect; app_head_swap.json ran with no unknown-node rejection; box crop followed a user resize; correct swap saved. Hyper = 4 steps. node_lock.json ready to commit at close.

Head Swap: allow the target/reference box to extend OUTSIDE the frame and auto-pad that edge, keeping the box square. Surfaced during MPI-324 Head Swap validation - an edge-adjacent head cannot capture full hair with an in-frame square without grabbing the neighbour (caused the first-run fail). Pad, do not shrink. Workflow/UX improvement, not a v1.2 blocker.
RunPod validation sweep umbrella (1.2.0 release gate): MPI-282, MPI-300, MPI-310, Head Swap. MPI-198 deferred.

node_lock MpiNodes pin bumped ba9e156 -> aaa1d2d (1.2.6 adds MpiBox/MpiBoxCrop/MpiBoxMask/MpiFromBox, pure superset). Code done; maturity validating pending Pod-connect proof the wrapper reinstalled the new commit. Folded into MPI-324 Phase 0.

Evaluate FLUX.2 Klein 4B (Apache 2.0) for masked object/text removal. Fill dev + Klein 9B ruled out non-commercial. Outpainting already solved via black-border + edit models. Cheaper pre-test: does Krea2 masked-edit (MPI-282) already remove on empty prompt?

Carded after a failed attempt on 2026-07-20 was fully reverted. Native Electron drag-out (startDrag) worked - real filenames, multi-file drop - but the e.preventDefault() it required killed the shared HTML5 dragstart that in-app drops ride on, so dragging cards inside the library stopped reaching the prompt and landed as imported cards. Reverted all three files to HEAD; tree clean, nothing shipped. Root process failure: skipped the Context Router (components.md / gallery.md / events.md) and never asked what else consumed that dragstart. Card instructs the next agent to read the docs FIRST. Open and unexplained: pressing Cancel on the Windows file-conflict dialog exited the app, with no trace in app.log because main.js has no render-process-gone or uncaughtException handler.

Test-first card: does RAW + a speed-up LoRA match the Turbo transformer? If yes, drop ~24.5GB of Turbo weights and move Krea2 to the Qwen-Edit runtime tier-radio shape. Negative-prompt gating is currently structural (cfg 1 discards it) and would be LOST in a single-card collapse - flagged in the card as a design decision.

Picked up. Decision: aggressive source-filter of ComfyUI stdout (user has terminal access for INFO detail), delete destructive startup line-trim, gitignore logs/.
